title: Shared definitions for blemeesd/2 wire frames
description: Reusable $defs referenced by the per-frame schemas. Not a frame schema itself.

SessionId.description: Client-owned session identifier. The daemon does not validate format at the protocol layer, but clients must supply a UUID (e.g. str(uuid.uuid4())) — the Claude backend forwards it verbatim to `claude -p --session-id`, which only accepts UUIDs. Codex uses its own threadId internally; for backend-neutrality always pass a UUID.

RequestId.description: Opaque correlation id set by the client on request frames; echoed on the reply.

Seq.description: Monotonic per-session sequence number injected by the daemon on session-stream frames.

PeerPid.description: Peer PID captured via SO_PEERCRED; may be absent on platforms that do not expose it.

Backend.description: Backend selector. Set on inbound blemeesd.open and echoed on every outbound agent.* frame so clients can switch by backend if they want to.

items.description: Content block (text, image, document, …). Schema is intentionally permissive so new block types pass through.

AgentUserMessage.description: Inbound user-turn message. Forwarded verbatim to Claude's stream-json stdin; for Codex, text blocks are concatenated into a `prompt` string and non-text blocks are rejected (see spec §5.5).

NormalisedUsage.description: Per-turn token usage, normalised across backends. See docs/agent-events.md for the source-event mapping. additionalProperties:true so future fields backends emit pass through unchanged.

reasoning_output_tokens.description: Codex-only; surfaced separately (not folded into output_tokens) so clients can budget reasoning vs. text independently.

SessionSummary.description: One row in a blemeesd.sessions reply. The shape merges disk-derived fields (`mtime_ms`, `size`, `preview`) with live-only fields (`title`, `model`, `cwd`, `started_at_ms`, `last_active_at_ms`, `owner_pid`, `last_seq`, `turn_active`). Disk-only rows lack the live fields; live-only rows lack the disk fields. Clients should treat every optional field as advisory — absence means "not known", never "zero".

mtime_ms.description: On-disk transcript mtime; absent for live-only rows.

size.description: On-disk transcript size in bytes; absent for live-only rows.

preview.description: Excerpt of the first user turn from the on-disk transcript; absent for live-only rows.

cwd.description: Working directory the session is rooted at.

model.description: Model id last reported by the backend (Claude `claude-sonnet-4-6`, Codex `gpt-5.2-codex`, etc.). Absent for sessions that haven't yet emitted `agent.system_init`.

title.description: Daemon-derived title from the first observed user message, capped at 80 characters. Absent for sessions that haven't driven a turn.

started_at_ms.description: Wall-clock when the session was first registered with the daemon. Live-only field.

last_active_at_ms.description: Wall-clock of the most recent `agent.result` (or `started_at_ms` if no turn has completed). Live-only field; clients should sort by this in preference to `mtime_ms` when both are present.

owner_pid.description: PID of the connection currently driving the session. Absent when the session is detached or when the OS does not expose peer credentials.

last_seq.description: Highest seq the session has produced. Live-only field; matches the `last_seq` carried on `blemeesd.opened` and `blemeesd.watching`.

turn_active.description: True iff the session has a turn in flight (between `agent.user` send and `agent.result` receive). Live-only field.
